message:
test(fcp-core): pin InvokeContext serde shape (flywheel_connectors-kqfbb)

Bead asks for ConnectorContext serde JSON+CBOR roundtrip. fcp-core
has no type literally named ConnectorContext; the per-request context
that travels with connector invocations is InvokeContext
(protocol.rs:410), the documented context for i18n (locale),
pagination, and distributed tracing (trace_id + request_tags). 4
fields, all with skip_serializing_if (3 Optional + 1 HashMap-empty).

Existing inline tests in invoke_golden_vectors.rs::invoke_context
pinned Default and a handful of fields. This test pins the gaps:

- Default empty context serializes to `{}` since every field has
  skip_serializing_if (Option::is_none for 3 Optional, HashMap::is_empty
  for request_tags)
- Per-field skip_serializing_if shape: Some/None distinction on every
  Optional field; empty HashMap omitted vs non-empty present
- Default values when deserializing partial JSON: missing fields
  default to None / empty
- JSON + CBOR round-trip preserves all 4 fields for default and
  populated contexts
- Multi-byte UTF-8 locale (日本語 / 中文 / kanji + emoji) preserved
  through both JSON and CBOR
- Pagination is free-form serde_json::Value: object / array / string
  forms all round-trip
- Cross-format consistency: JSON and CBOR decode to same context
- Distinct locale / trace_id / request_tags axes produce distinct
  JSON serializations
- Empty-string locale (Some("")) is distinct from None on the wire
  (Some emits field with empty string, None omits field entirely)
- Empty vs populated context produces distinct serializations

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
